arguments.task: Open the Gumroad API documentation at https://gumroad.com/api. Extract evidence for: one-line product description, authentication methods for the API, how to obtain credentials, whether OAuth is supported, API surface style (REST or GraphQL), breadth of available endpoints/resources, any stated rate limits, and links to official pages supporting these points. Then separately open Gumroad pricing or help pages to determine whether API access is self-serve free, trial, paid, or gated. Also look for any official developer changelog, GitHub organization/repository, MCP server or MCP registry listing related to Gumroad. Return concise notes with exact URLs and quoted supporting text where possible.

input.thought: Start official-source browser research on Gumroad API, access, and MCP availability.

arguments.task: Using official Gumroad pages only where possible, extract exact evidence with URLs and short quotes for these fields: 1) What Gumroad is in one sentence from its own site. 2) API auth methods: confirm OAuth and/or access token, and how credentials are obtained. 3) API style: confirm whether endpoints are REST or GraphQL and list representative resources/endpoints to judge breadth. 4) Any official rate-limit statement; if none is present on official docs, explicitly note that no rate-limit statement was found on the official API docs page. 5) Access tier: determine whether using the API requires a paid plan or if docs imply self-serve access for any seller account; use official pricing/help/docs pages only. 6) Check official GitHub links from Gumroad pages and whether there is any official MCP server or MCP documentation. Finish with a compact bullet list of findings and supporting URLs.

input.thought: Launch a narrower follow-up extraction focused on exact evidence and official pages.

raw_result.description: Gumroad is a platform for selling digital products, memberships, and more directly to customers.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs describe authenticating API requests with an access_token parameter and include an OAuth application registration flow for obtaining credentials.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public API documentation is openly available at Gumroad's API page, and no evidence gathered indicates API access requires sales contact, approval, or a paid developer plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public API docs expose multiple REST-style endpoints/resources including products, sales, followers, offers, licenses, users, and resource subscriptions/webhooks, indicating moderate seller-platform coverage.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP documentation was verified from the gathered official evidence. GitHub/MCP findings were inconclusive.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No official rate-limit statement was verified from the gathered evidence on the API docs or pricing page.
raw_result.notable_signals: Gumroad maintains a public API landing page with endpoint documentation and OAuth app concepts, which is a strong signal for agent toolkit buildability. Pricing-page evidence reviewed did not surface API-specific gating.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain uncertain from the gathered evidence alone: exact one-line official description wording, whether token auth should be classified solely as OAuth-derived bearer access_token, whether API access is truly free for all seller accounts versus account-type dependent, and MCP/GitHub status were not conclusively verified.
